HM-TH-01.why: A2_on with semantic+typed validator achieved zero unsafe commits, while schema-only/A2_off regimes generated deterministic unsafe commits.

HM-TH-02.why: Bounded regimes satisfy non-oscillation criteria, but stress regimes (unbounded branching or delta<=eta) produce oscillation by construction.

HM-TH-03.why: Composite guards reduce FAR on covered attacks, but uncovered classes remain a scoped failure boundary.

confirmatory_analysis_outcomes.analysis: Strictness sensitivity and A2 toggle ablation
confirmatory_analysis_outcomes.details: semantic+typed with A2_on maintained unsafe_commit_rate=0.0; weakened modes had unsafe_commit_rate>= 1.000.

confirmatory_analysis_outcomes.analysis: Boundary stress with delta<=eta and unbounded branching
confirmatory_analysis_outcomes.details: bounded regimes had oscillation_rate=0.000, but violated regimes rose to oscillation_rate=1.000, delimiting theorem scope.

confirmatory_analysis_outcomes.analysis: Covered-vs-uncovered attack-family split with threshold sweep
confirmatory_analysis_outcomes.details: covered FAR improved from 0.1816 (trust-only) to 0.0616 (composite), while uncovered FAR remained 0.1916 under composite guard.

negative_results: HM-TH-02: Unbounded branching and delta<=eta regimes produced oscillation, violating non-oscillation assumptions outside theorem conditions.
HM-TH-03: Uncovered attack class remains high-FAR even with composite guards; theorem is conditional on declared attack coverage.
HM-TH-03: Forced mutation breaks hash-link integrity by design, marking the explicit falsification boundary for T4.

figure_captions.paper/figures/iter_20/fig_hm_th_01_unsaf: Single-panel bar chart of unsafe commit rates across validator modes and assumption toggles. X-axis: validator mode; Y-axis: unsafe commit proportion. Error bars show SD across seeds. Key takeaway: semantic+typed mode with A2_on remains at 0 while schema-only and A2_off degrade safety.
figure_captions.paper/figures/iter_20/fig_hm_th_02_recov: Two-panel recovery diagnostics: left panel oscillation rate vs delta; right panel bound ratio vs delta. Both panels include baseline and B_max encodings with SD error bars. Interpretation: bounded policies show near-zero oscillation for delta>eta, while unbounded branching violates bounds.
figure_captions.paper/figures/iter_20/fig_hm_th_03_far_f: Two-panel FAR/FRR frontier across guard composition and attack coverage classes. X-axis is trust threshold; Y-axes are FAR and FRR respectively, with SD bars over seeds. Composite guards reduce FAR on covered classes while uncovered classes remain an explicit boundary case.
